title: Lightly Grilled Greek Lamb Souvlaki
description: A rich and crumbly Greek dish featuring lamb souvlaki, perfect for a brunch mood, with a hint of blue food coloring for a unique touch, all while being keto-friendly and cooked to perfection using a bain-marie, although the blue food coloring is used sparingly for presentation and does not affect the keto-friendly aspect of the dish
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, approximately 400 degrees Fahrenheit, ensuring a safe internal temperature for cooking lamb
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, dried oregano, salt, and pepper to create a marinade
Add the lamb cubes to the marinade and toss to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to penetrate the meat
Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece to facilitate even cooking
Grill the lamb souvlaki for 8-10 minutes per side, or until cooked to an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit for medium-rare, ensuring food safety
While the lamb is cooking, prepare a bain-marie by filling a large saucepan with water to a depth of about 2 inches and bringing it to a simmer over low heat
Once the lamb is cooked, place the skewers in the bain-marie, cover, and let cook for an additional 5 minutes to keep warm and maintain a safe internal temperature
Remove the skewers from the bain-marie and garnish with a sprinkle of blue food coloring, if desired, for a unique and visually appealing presentation
Serve the lamb souvlaki hot, garnished with additional lemon wedges and a side of roasted vegetables or a salad, if desired
